title: EdenFinTech Stock Scan — CPS, AAP, DORM, PYPL
executive_summary: 0 stocks survived analysis out of 4 scanned. No investable candidates at current prices.

DORM eliminated at Step 1 (only 34% off ATH vs 60% required). CPS and AAP eliminated by 100% mechanical downside (negative trough FCF margins) and sub-30% CAGR. PYPL eliminated by epistemic confidence filter (confidence 1/5 due to CEO instability, active litigation, and regulatory risk).

Key finding: CPS and AAP both have negative historical FCF margins in their 5yr trough, producing $0 mechanical floors. Neither currently meets the methodology's minimum investability requirements despite ongoing turnaround narratives.
PYPL has the strongest fundamentals ($5.6B FCF, growing revenue) but governance dysfunction (3 CEOs in 3 years) and active litigation create unacceptably high epistemic uncertainty.
portfolio_impact: CPS (~25% weight), AAP (~8%), PYPL (~11-20%) are all current holdings that failed this scan
This scan does NOT trigger sell signals for existing holdings — scan rejection means 'do not add new capital', not 'sell existing position'. Existing positions are governed by Step 8 holding reviews.
Auto parts concentration (~33%) remains elevated. Both CPS and AAP showing 100% mechanical downside is a portfolio-level risk signal worth monitoring.
PYPL holding review (2026-03-07) recommended HOLD_AND_MONITOR with mid-2026 deadline for CEO Lores to show credible strategy. This scan's epistemic confidence result (1/5) reinforces that timeline.
CPS holding review (2026-03-07) recommended HOLD. Target needs revision from $193 to ~$62-75 (25-33% CAGR from current $32).
Deployment recommendation: Scenario 1 applies (2 slots available with cash). No new candidates identified. Hold cash for better opportunities.

rejected_at_screening.reason: Only 34.3% off ATH ($166.92 ATH vs $109.33 current). Needs 60%+ decline to qualify. Dorman is a quality auto parts company but not beaten down enough.

rejected_at_analysis_detail_packets.rejection_reason: Multiple failures: (1) Base case CAGR 25.1% below 30% hurdle, (2) Base probability 50% below 60% hard cap, (3) 100% mechanical downside (negative trough FCF margins), (4) Score 1.27 below 45 watchlist threshold. Does not qualify for 20% CAGR exception (CEO not top-tier, runway <6yr).

multiple.source: OEM auto parts baseline per sector knowledge

rejected_at_analysis_detail_packets.thesis_summary: CPS is an OEM auto parts manufacturer emerging from Chapter 11 bankruptcy (2020) with improving margins. Management targets 10% EBITDA margin by FY2026. However, FCF margins remain below 1%, equity is negative, leverage is extreme, and the EV transition creates structural uncertainty for ICE-focused suppliers.

structural_diagnosis.upgrade_trigger: Sustained FCF margin >3% for 2+ quarters AND equity turns positive
structural_diagnosis.thesis_killer: Covenant violation, another restructuring, major OEM customer loss, or failure to refinance 2031 debt at reasonable rates

rejected_at_analysis_detail_packets.rejection_reason: Multiple failures: (1) Conservative base case CAGR 21.4% below 30% hurdle, (2) Base probability 50% below 60% hard cap, (3) 100% mechanical downside (negative trough FCF margins), (4) Score 0.71-2.88 below 45 watchlist threshold. Does not qualify for 20% CAGR exception (runway <6yr).

base_case.discount_path: Aftermarket retail baseline 18x -> revenue decline -3x -> leverage -2x -> execution risk -1x -> 12x

multiple.source: Aftermarket retail baseline with heavy discounts

rejected_at_analysis_detail_packets.thesis_summary: AAP is an aftermarket auto parts retailer undergoing massive restructuring under CEO Shane O'Kelly. Worldpac sale provided $1.5B+ cash, store closures underway, targeting 7% adjusted OI margin by FY2027. However, FCF has been negative for 2 of last 3 years, revenue declining for 4 consecutive years, and execution risk is extreme given the depth of the turnaround needed.

structural_diagnosis.upgrade_trigger: FCF turns positive for 2 consecutive quarters AND revenue decline stabilizes
structural_diagnosis.thesis_killer: Continued FCF burn through FY2026, covenant breach on elevated debt, or loss of key store-level economics

rejected_at_analysis_detail_packets.rejection_reason: Epistemic confidence filter: base 60% x 0.50 multiplier = 30% effective probability, below 60% threshold. Confidence 1/5 (3 No answers + Legal/Investigation friction -2). Score drops from 48.92 (pre-epistemic) to 36.92. Position size 0% (confidence 1 = watchlist only).

base_case.discount_path: Payment processor baseline 15x -> legal risk -2x -> branded checkout decel -1x -> 12x

tbv_crosscheck.flag: Floor > 2x TBV/share — acceptable for asset-light payments business where value is in network, not tangible assets

probability.ceilings_applied: CEO tenure <1yr -> 65% cap -> rounds down to 60% band
probability.threshold_proximity_warning: Base probability 60% is AT the 60% hard cap — review for threshold anchoring

q1_operational.justification: Legal/governance risk dominates — CEO firing pattern suggests board-level dysfunction, wrongful termination lawsuit
q1_operational.evidence: Chriss fired after 16mo, Schulman pushed out, Lores from HP hardware

q2_regulatory.justification: CFPB oversight, state MTLs, Honey DOJ investigation
q2_regulatory.evidence: CFPB BNPL scrutiny, DOJ Honey investigation reports

q3_precedent.justification: Credit services turnaround base rate ~80%, Block/Square recovered from 88% drawdown

q4_nonbinary.justification: CEO instability creates binary outcome — Lores stabilizes or another change triggers further destruction

q5_macro.justification: Primarily US-focused digital payments, domestic execution drives turnaround

epistemic_confidence.friction_note: Full friction applied — active Chriss lawsuit is distinct from Q1 governance dysfunction and Q2 regulatory oversight

rejected_at_analysis_detail_packets.probability_sensitivity_note: All bands produce effective probability <60% at confidence 1 (multiplier 0.50). PYPL needs confidence >=3 AND base probability >=70% to pass the epistemic filter.
rejected_at_analysis_detail_packets.thesis_summary: PayPal is a dominant digital payments platform (434M accounts, $33.3B revenue) trading 85% off ATH due to CEO instability (3rd CEO in 3 years), active litigation, and branded checkout deceleration. The turnaround depends on new CEO Lores stabilizing leadership and executing on margin expansion. Fundamentals are strong ($5.6B FCF, growing revenue, fortress balance sheet) but governance risk makes the outcome unacceptably uncertain.

structural_diagnosis.upgrade_trigger: CEO Lores demonstrates credible fintech strategy by mid-2026, Chriss lawsuit resolved, AND 2 quarters of branded checkout stabilization. This would likely raise confidence to 3+ and base probability to 70%, producing effective prob >=60%.
structural_diagnosis.thesis_killer: Another CEO change, Chriss lawsuit revealing deeper governance failures, or branded checkout market share loss accelerating below 5% growth

current_holding_overlays.reason: 100% mechanical downside and 50% probability reject new capital. However, existing position (avg cost ~$14) has different risk profile — already up >100%. Holding review (2026-03-07) recommended HOLD. Monitor FCF margin trajectory toward 3%+ target and equity recovery. Consider Step 8 review if margins stall.

100% mechanical downside and 50% probability reject new capital. Existing position (avg cost ~$40.61) is currently +24% but restructuring execution is unproven. FCF has been negative 2 of 3 years. Monitor FY2026 quarterly results for FCF inflection. If FCF remains negative through Q2 FY2026, trigger Step 8 review.

Epistemic confidence filter (1/5) rejects new capital. Existing position (avg cost $41.10) is approximately breakeven. Holding review (2026-03-07) recommended HOLD_AND_MONITOR with mid-2026 deadline for Lores to show credible strategy. This scan reinforces that timeline — if no strategic clarity by mid-2026, downgrade to EXIT consideration.

methodology_notes: Qualitative assessments (moats, management, probability) are LLM estimates — review critically
Valuation multiples involve judgment — verify reasoning matches your own assessment
Catalyst timelines are estimates based on public information as of scan date
All scoring math computed via calc-score.sh (deterministic, not LLM-generated)
Epistemic confidence assessed independently — reviewer approach applied without seeing probability or score
PCS answers are evidence-anchored — each answer cites a source or declares NO_EVIDENCE
Risk-type friction applied: PYPL Legal/Investigation -> -2 (raw 2/5 -> adjusted 1/5)
Threshold proximity warning: PYPL base probability 60% is AT the 60% hard cap

Downside methodology audited: floor calc present for all candidates, negative trough margins produce $0 floors for CPS and AAP, TBV cross-check complete for PYPL (2.65x flag noted, acceptable for asset-light)
Calibration rules checked: no exceptions triggered (growth_revenue_bound: PYPL CAGR 9.2% < 15% threshold; margin_outlier: all gaps <8pp)
No multiple consistency audit needed — no ranked candidates
Finding ZERO recommendable stocks is a valid and valuable outcome. Patience is an edge.
